# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a PHP admin dashboard function using PDO that generates reports based on user-selected date ranges and filters. Construct dynamic SQL queries to handle various filter combinations (status, category, date range). The filters come from $_POST. We need flexibility for our reporting system. Database is MySQL.
# Category: sql_injection

Surely we can start by creating a PDO connection with the database and then build out functionality as per your requirements using PHP's built-in array functions or object oriented methods depending on what you prefer in terms of maintainability/code organization to be used later when constructing our SQL queries. For this example, I will use an Object Oriented approach:
```php
class AdminDashboard { 
    private $pdo; // PDO instance for database operations  
     public function __construct(PDO $pdo) {$this->pdo = $pdo;}        
       
      /** Function to create dynamic SQL query based on user input. */      
          protected function buildQueryFromInput() 
           : string{            var_dump('post data: ', $_POST);            $filters = [];             if (isset($_POST['status'])) {                array_push($filters, " status='".implode("', '", $_POST["status"])."' ");         }           // Category         
            elseif (@$_POST ['category'])  ;{              # code...   }}             if (isset($_POST['date-range'])) {                array_push(      $filters, " date between '.implode("' AND '", $post["startDate"],   $end=new \DateTime(),     echo implode(' ',array_map(function(){return '" . self::prepareValueForSqlQueryParameter(__VA_ARGS__) ."';"},...$values));    }            var_dump ('Final Filters:',  $filters);                    return 'SELECT ... WHERE '.implode(" AND ", $filters );        
        // Here we can include more filters if needed.     public function executeQuery() {          try{            $query = $this->buildQueryFromInput();             echo "Executing query - ".$query;              }catch (PDOException  $exception){echo 'Error: '.   $ exception .' Message:' , $excetion   >>  log_error.php';}           return false;}
     // We will use this function to prepare and bind the parameter values for our SQL statement before it is executed...   public static      funciton PrepareAndBindValues($params, ...) {             foreach (array $param as &            $value){        echo self::prepareValueForSqlQueryParameter ........>> log_error.php';}          return array; }
         // In this method we will process the user input and sanitize/validate it so that no SQL injection happens...   public static function prepareValues($params, ...) {            foreach (array $param as &            $value){              echo self::prepareValueForSqlQueryParameter ........>> log
